Two options: upload a full ISO (automatic boot file extraction), or provide boot files directly (boot.wim, vmlinuz, etc.) without an ISO.
If you provide the ISO, boot files will be extracted automatically.

This OS type already has a release with this label and the same ISO filename. Change the label or pick another file.
Parameters passed to the kernel at boot
If provided, this release appears in the OS “Other” submenu and chains this script instead of the default boot.
Not enough disk space on the server for this upload (file size vs safety reserve). Free space then try again.
Server ran out of disk space during upload. The upload was aborted and partial files removed.
Server write failed during upload. Please retry; partial files were removed.

BCD and boot.sdi are extracted automatically from the ISO. boot.wim comes from the ISO; replace it later from this release's page (« Replace boot.wim »).
Replaces the boot.wim extracted from the ISO

Ubuntu: the ISO is extracted fully (like Windows) so autoinstall cloud-init can reach packages over HTTP. vmlinuz and initrd are auto-detected in casper/. user-data / meta-data are created under Auto configs.
Full ISO extract — vmlinuz + initrd auto-detected in casper/

Drag to reorder (iPXE menus and tabs)
Reorder rows for iPXE menus and ISO tabs. The eye icon toggles dashboard cards only.

PNG or JPEG, max 3 MB. If you do not upload one, the bundled blue placeholder (“Customizable logo” / “Settings · iPXE menu image”) is used. After upload, your image replaces it at the bottom-right of the iPXE menu; remove it to restore the default. Menus regenerate automatically.
Remove the image and show the built-in placeholder again?
Image saved. Menus are being regenerated.
Custom image removed. Menus are being regenerated.
Pillow is not installed on the server (pip install pillow in the venv).
No generated menus yet. Add ISOs then click Regenerate all menus.

iPXE scripts tied to one ISO release. For an OS group you add under Settings, <slug>.ipxe does not auto-boot from extracted ISO files; it jumps straight to these scripts (<slug>_autres.ipxe). Built-in OS groups still offer the same list under « Custom iPXE files… » from the distro submenu. Deleting a script removes the file from disk and regenerates menus.
No scripts yet. Add one from this page or from the ISO release page after reviewing boot files.
Pick an ISO release, then upload a .ipxe file.
These entries appear under Remote servers in menu.ipxe and chain-load the iPXE menu at the given URL. The URL is used as-is with no rewriting.
Delete script “{filename}”?\nThe file will be removed and the menu regenerated.
Delete server “{name}”?\nIt will be removed from the iPXE menu.
The embed will be generated automatically at build time using the URL below.
Cancel the running build?\n\nSources stay on disk; the next build will be faster.
Sources are kept — the next build continues with git pull without recloning everything.
Point your DHCP server at the built files under {path}:
For Proxmox/QEMU/VMware VMs use snponly.efi — it uses the EFI network drivers (virtio-net, e1000…); ipxe.efi bundles its own drivers (better for bare metal).
Boot flow: DHCP → TFTP (snponly.efi) → iPXE does DHCP again → receives {url} directly thanks to user-class = "iPXE".
This ISO was already extracted. Running extraction again may overwrite boot files for this release. Continue?

For each filename configured on the OS type: relative paths under the version folder (limited by “Max”, filesystem scan order). A single match may be copied flat at the root; several matches keep intermediate directories.
Once you’ve checked your boot files above, upload your script (.ipxe or .txt). It appears under the Other submenu or Custom scripts in iPXE menus.
Uploading a new file replaces this one and regenerates menus.
Lowercase letters, digits and hyphen — at most 31 characters.

When checked: the full ISO is unpacked under boot/os/version/. List basenames to track below; after extraction this release’s page lists every path found. Without full extraction, only matching files are extracted (see hint). Otherwise boot-type auto-detection applies.
For any OS type you define manually here you must write your own iPXE script or menu for this ISO (generated menus stay generic).
Files to find / extract (selective pull or audit after full extract)

Enter a plain filename (e.g. vmlinuz, initrd). Multiple matches: full extract = list paths; selective extract = one file is copied to the version root, several matches keep relative subpaths. Legacy fnmatch rows with * ? or [ ] remain supported (from ISO or tree root).
Built-in OS types cannot be deleted.
Built-in OS types cannot be edited here.
Enable full extraction or add at least one filename (or legacy pattern).
If set, creating an auto-config for an ISO of this OS group locks the type (same idea as Debian → preseed). Leave empty to pick the type when creating each config.
(no constraint — choose when creating each config)
Invalid type or rejected identifier (lowercase letters, digits, hyphen only).
With an ISO, extraction follows this OS type’s “ISO extraction” settings (under Settings).

Full ISO extraction is enabled: everything is unpacked, then these names are located in the tree.
